title: Garnished Asian Vietnamese Rice Noodle Fresh Spring Rolls
description: Fermented and molten, these fresh spring rolls are a delicious Vietnamese dish that is perfect for meal prep. Use Asian-chive-buchu and namul-blanch-season for an authentic flavor.
instructions: Soak rice noodles in warm water for 10 minutes, then drain and cut into 4-inch pieces.
Combine Asian-chive-buchu, namul-blanch-season, and 1 tablespoon water in a bowl. Mix well to create a fermented paste.
Add the fermented paste to the rice noodles and toss to coat.
Heat a small skillet over medium heat and add 1 tablespoon oil. Add the rice noodle mixture and stir-fry for 5 minutes, breaking up any clumps.
Prepare fresh spring roll wrappers by soaking them in warm water for 5 minutes.
Fill a large bowl with warm water and have it ready to dip the spring roll wrappers in.

To assemble the spring rolls, place a rice noodle mixture onto the center of a spring roll wrapper, then fold the bottom half up over the filling, followed by the top half, and finally the sides to seal the roll.
Place the spring roll on a slate tile and garnish with chopped scallions, cilantro, and sesame seeds. Repeat with the remaining ingredients.
Serve immediately, or store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and serve chilled.
To reheat, microwave for 20-30 seconds or pan-fry until crispy and serve hot.
Serve with your favorite dipping sauce, such as peanut sauce or sweet chili sauce.
